Finding Content

The XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable function is designed to be used by both full-function online titles and by online titles that provide only simple content download with the content download manager. In either case, XOnlineOfferingIsNewContentAvailable indicates whether new content that matches specified bit flags is available. Content is considered "new" if it was not available the last time the title enumerated available offerings (or launched the downloader). As a result, a title can perform a check for new content (at startup or later during actual gameplay) without requiring the user to log onto Xbox Live. A title can also perform a check for new content without actually requiring the user to purchase an Xbox Live account. If new content is available, the title can present the user with a new or emphasized download content option that will either enumerate available offerings or launch the downloader. This frees players from repeatedly checking for new content to download, only to find none available.

Titles using the content download manager, after launching the downloader, need perform no further online work or calls. Titles that do not use the content download manager must perform the enumeration steps described next.

  • Enumerating Offerings
  • Offering Details

Enumerating Offerings

Games call XOnlineOfferingEnumerate to enumerate available offerings. Results are returned in batches until all available offerings have been enumerated.

When calling XOnlineOfferingEnumerate, the title specifies the logged-on user for whom the offerings will be enumerated, along with the types of offerings to be enumerated. Only registered users (not guests) can enumerate offerings. The title can specify that only subscription or content offerings (or both) be enumerated. It can also restrict the enumeration to offerings that have certain title-specific flags set. There can be multiple descriptions for each available offering (for example, one for each supported language). The title can specify an index to determine which description should be retrieved for each offering. The number and meaning of the various descriptions is title-specific and is provided when the offerings are initially submitted. Except for the user and the device type to be enumerated, titles specify these enumeration options in an XONLINEOFFERING_ENUM_PARAMS structure passed to the XOnlineOfferingEnumerate function.

XOnlineOfferingEnumerate requires a buffer to use internally while the enumeration is being processed. The title can either allocate a sufficiently large buffer and pass it to XOnlineOfferingEnumerate, or it can allow the function to allocate a buffer internally. Use the XOnlineOfferingEnumerateMaxSize function to determine the buffer size needed to contain a specified number of enumeration records. For example, if the title wishes to receive at most ten offerings in each batch, it can specify that number when calling XOnlineOfferingEnumerateMaxSize. The required buffer size that XOnlineOfferingEnumerateMaxSize returns is large enough to hold ten offerings, and each subsequent batch of returned results contains at most ten records.

XOnlineOfferingEnumerate creates an asynchronous online task and passes a handle to that task back to the title. The return value of XOnlineOfferingEnumerate indicates the success or failure of the task creation, not of the entire enumeration operation. At regular intervals, the title calls XOnlineTaskContinue to perform work on the task. XOnlineTaskContinue returns XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING until the task is complete, partial results are available, or an error has occurred.

Note    The technique of creating an asynchronous task and polling for the results is often referred to as "pumping" the task handle.

When XOnlineTaskContinue returns the values XONLINETASK_S_SUCCESS or XONLINETASK_S_RESULTS_AVAIL, results are available for your title. XOnlineOfferingEnumerateGetResults retrieves a batch of available results in an array of pointers to XONLINEOFFERING_INFO structures. Each structure in the array contains the information for a single offering. The title should not free (or otherwise manipulate) the buffers containing the offering information. The pfMoreResults parameter of XOnlineOfferingEnumerateGetResults is used to obtain a value indicating whether or not more results are available. If more results are available, the title again calls XOnlineTaskContinue until it returns a value other than XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING. At that time, the title calls XOnlineOfferingEnumerateGetResults to retrieve the next batch of results. When XOnlineOfferingEnumerateGetResults indicates that no more results are available, the title closes the asynchronous task handle by calling XOnlineTaskClose. Additionally, if the title allocated a buffer for XOnlineOfferingEnumerate, it frees that buffer when the results no longer need to be accessed.

If XOnlineTaskContinue returns an error code, some problem was encountered while the enumeration was being performed. The task handle should be closed with XOnlineTaskClose. If the title allocated a buffer to pass to XOnlineOfferingEnumerate, it should free that buffer. The title can reattempt enumeration. However, it's usually best to query the user before doing so.

Offering Details

When offerings are enumerated and displayed to the user, each offering has only a simple description associated with it that briefly describes the content or subscription. Titles present the full details for the offering, including the purchase price and an extended description, only after the user selects an offering from the list. The user can then see the full offering information and choose whether to purchase the offering.

Use XOnlineOfferingDetails to retrieve the pricing and detailed description information for an offering. XOnlineOfferingDetails accepts a unique offering identifier (an XOFFERING_ID), which is part of the information returned for each offering that was enumerated.

Like XOnlineOfferingEnumerate, XOnlineOfferingDetails requires a buffer for storing the details information while that information is being retrieved. The title can either allocate a sufficiently large buffer itself or allow XOnlineOfferingDetails to do so internally. Call XOnlineOfferingDetailsMaxSize to calculate the proper buffer size needed to store the details information.

Also like XOnlineOfferingEnumerate, XOnlineOfferingDetails does not immediately retrieve and return the details results. Instead, XOnlineOfferingDetails creates an asynchronous online task whose handle must be pumped for results. Call XOnlineOfferingDetailsGetResults to obtain the results of the task. As mentioned previously, the details information contains the pricing and extended description of the offering. The pricing information is returned in an XONLINE_PRICE structure that can be converted to a display string by the XOnlineOfferingPriceFormat function.

If the XOnlineOfferingDetails function allocated its own details buffer internally (rather than the title passing in an allocated buffer), the internal buffer is deallocated and becomes invalid when your title calls XOnlineTaskClose. As a result, the details information returned by XOnlineOfferingDetailsGetResults in the pDetailsBuffer parameter becomes invalid as well. Therefore, the title should read (and copy if necessary) the returned details data before closing the details task. If the title allocated its own buffer to pass to XOnlineOfferingDetails, the returned details information becomes invalid when the title frees its own allocated buffer.
